message: Initial commit: project structure, database models, rate limiting, instrumentation

message: Implement authentication middleware with bcrypt-secured API keys (TK-9E0440)
author: Alex (Senior Eng)

message: Implement budget enforcement and audit logging middleware (TK-1DD2DC, TK-B8260A)
author: Casey (DevOps)

message:
Implement complete LLM proxy endpoint with OpenAI integration (TK-F87FF4)

- Add openai and tiktoken dependencies to requirements.txt
- Implement full proxy logic: OpenAI SDK integration, token counting, cost calculation
- Populate request.state.llm_request_data for audit logging middleware
- Add OPENAI_API_KEY to config for provider authentication
- Proper error handling for provider failures and token counting errors

Data contract for audit logging (Casey):
  request.state.llm_request_data contains:
    - customer_id, timestamp, model, endpoint
    - tokens_input, tokens_output (NOTE: not tokens_in/tokens_out)
    - cost_input_usd, cost_output_usd, pricing_version
    - response_status, response_latency_ms, throttled
    - rate_limit_remaining, rate_limit_reset_at
    - budget_spent_usd, budget_limit_usd

Jordan: Update support SQL queries to use tokens_input/tokens_output column names.
author: Alex (Senior Eng)

message: Fix budget/audit middleware to match LLMRequest schema field names
author: Casey (DevOps)

message:
Add throttle_reason field to proxy data contract (TK-F87FF4)

- Add throttle_reason to request.state.llm_request_data (set to None when not throttled)
- Ensures data contract matches LLMRequest schema completely
- Budget/rate limit middleware should populate this field when throttling occurs

Per Priya's architectural review feedback.
author: Alex (Senior Eng)

message:
Add OpenAI mock for integration testing and validate middleware flow

- Mock OpenAI client for testing without external API dependencies
- Integration test validates: auth → budget check → proxy → audit write
- Confirms budget enforcement blocks over-limit requests correctly
- Confirms audit logging writes to llm_requests with correct schema fields
- Both under-budget and over-budget scenarios tested successfully

Production deployment will need OPENAI_API_KEY configured in secrets.
author: Casey (DevOps)

message:
Configure OpenAI API key in production secrets (TK-FAF6B5)

- Add Kubernetes Secret for OpenAI API key in production namespace
- Update deployment manifest to inject OPENAI_API_KEY as environment variable
- Use SealedSecret for GitOps compliance (encrypted in repo, decrypted at runtime)
- Update config.py to read OPENAI_API_KEY from environment

Production deployment will decrypt and mount the secret automatically.
Ready for production smoke test with real OpenAI integration.
author: Casey (DevOps)

message:
Add graceful degradation for audit logging failures (TK-B8260A)

- Wrap Postgres write in try/except to prevent customer-facing errors
- Log full request.state.llm_request_data to structured logs if DB write fails
- Move Redis budget update before Postgres write for data consistency
- Ensure customer gets 200 response even if audit logging fails
- Add audit_write_failure metric to track degraded state

Per Priya's architectural review: if proxy succeeds but audit write fails,
we must not fail the customer request. Structured logs provide fallback
for billing reconciliation.

Production smoke test should validate this path with simulated DB failure.
author: Casey (DevOps)

message:
Add basic audit write error handling for beta launch (TK-FAF6B5)

- Wrap Postgres write in try/except to prevent customer-facing errors
- Log complete request.state.llm_request_data as JSON on write failure
- Move Redis budget update BEFORE Postgres write for data consistency
- Customer gets 200 response even if audit logging fails

Basic error handling for controlled beta (2-3 customers). Full graceful
degradation (automated alerting, deterministic UUIDs, reconstruction) ships
in TK-CAF42C before scaling beyond beta.

Per Marcus/Sarah/Priya scope decision: manual reconciliation from logs is
acceptable for beta phase.
author: Casey (DevOps)
